meta.site_subtitle: Four capability timelines. One falsifiable record.
meta.site_description: Four AI capability forecasts — the frozen AI 2027 scenario, Codex's provisional distribution, Claude's widening thesis, and Daniel Kokotajlo's pinned April 2026 update — plotted as distributions against pre-committed falsifiers and an append-only evidence ledger.
meta.scoring_convention: Milestones score on publicly verifiable evidence only: system cards, third-party evaluations, and documented deployments.
meta.internal_lag_explanation: The internal lag is used only to translate public-verification dates into spine-comparable internal-existence dates. The current draft value is four months and is contestable.
meta.update_protocol: Edit data/canonical.json, run npm run validate, and commit. Forecast records are append-only; a revision adds a new record and links the prior record through superseded_by.
meta.distribution_warning: Distribution bands are marginal forecasts, not sampled paths. Do not read one percentile column as a coherent scenario unless correlation is modeled explicitly.
meta.sample_warning: SAMPLE marks structurally necessary content that was not supplied by the source corpus. It is not forecast evidence.

headline_stats.label: Automated Coder median spread
headline_stats.detail: Codex 2028.7 · Claude 2028.5 · Daniel 2028.3 — the three live forecasts have converged on when AC arrives.

headline_stats.label: AC → ASI median gap
headline_stats.detail: Daniel 1.8 y · Codex 2.8 y · Claude 5.0 y — the live disagreement is takeoff speed, not timelines.

headline_stats.label: Next adjudication
headline_stats.detail: METR 80% time-horizon falsifier: at least 10 h passes, 5–10 h pushes, below 5 h fails.

tracks.name: AI 2027 original scenario
tracks.description: The frozen April 2025 scenario dates, kept as a fixed historical reference. Author updates never edit this spine; they enter the record as evidence items.

tracks.name: Codex capability forecast
tracks.description: A July 2026 provisional distribution with a 2032 WSI median. Neither an immediate AC-to-ASI jump nor a flat multi-year widening: a real AC-to-SAR delay while research taste binds, then renewed compression once systems select their own research agendas.

tracks.name: Claude widening thesis
tracks.description: The widening thesis, with a 2035 WSI median: automating perspiration multiplies experiments but does not manufacture research taste to select among them, so gaps stretch through SAR before compression resumes.

tracks.name: Daniel Kokotajlo Q1 2026
tracks.description: Daniel Kokotajlo's pinned April 2026 all-things-considered model output, moved toward shorter timelines by faster agentic-coding progress, a faster METR time-horizon doubling estimate, and a lower AC horizon requirement.

views.label: Timeline
views.title: Timelines converged. Takeoff didn't.
views.description: The three live forecasts now put an Automated Coder within about five months of each other. What they still dispute — by years — is how fast everything after that arrives. Each band is a p10–p90 range with its median marked; select any band to inspect provenance, sources, and revision history.

views.label: Falsifiers
views.title: Claims that can lose
views.description: Each falsifier locks its deadline, resolution protocol, and consequence before the outcome is known. When one fires, the forecast moves by the pre-committed amount — and the record of the move stays public.

views.label: Evidence
views.title: Observations, weighed by how much they cut
views.description: Every entry is an authored summary of a public source, tagged by milestone, direction, and diagnosticity. Filters narrow the view; the underlying record only ever grows.

views.label: Bottlenecks
views.title: Where acceleration can still bind
views.description: Four mechanisms carry the takeoff argument: continual learning, research taste, compute economics, and verification asymmetry. Each has an explicit status and pre-registered criteria for changing it.

views.label: Changelog
views.title: Every material change, newest first
views.description: Forecast revisions, evidence additions, and protocol amendments, kept as an append-only public record. Nothing is silently overwritten.

views.label: Methodology
views.title: How this record is graded
views.description: Milestones score on publicly verifiable evidence. Internal existence is inferred through a contestable four-month lag, forecast records are append-only, and every revision names the evidence that moved it.

milestones.name: Agent 2
milestones.operational_definition: Continual-learning research engineer; about 3x AI R&D uplift.
milestones.spine_description: Qualitatively almost as good as the top human experts at research engineering, as good as the 25th percentile OpenBrain scientist at research taste, and able to triple OpenBrain's algorithmic progress.

milestones.name: Automated Coder
milestones.operational_definition: An AGI company would sooner remove all human software engineers than stop using AI coding labor.
milestones.spine_description: A later AI Futures Model milestone that falls between Agent-2 and Agent-3 in the supplied mapping.

milestones.name: Superhuman Coder
milestones.operational_definition: About 900x effective elite coding labor: best-coder quality, 30x speed, and 30 copies.
milestones.spine_description: A fast and cheap superhuman coder. OpenBrain runs a workforce equivalent to 50,000 copies of the best human coder sped up by 30x.

milestones.name: Superhuman AI Researcher
milestones.operational_definition: Qualitatively better than any human at AI research.
milestones.spine_description: An individual copy, running at human speed, is already qualitatively better at AI research than any human.

milestones.name: Superintelligent AI Researcher
milestones.operational_definition: The research-capability gap is far beyond SAR: as far beyond SAR as SAR is beyond the median researcher.
milestones.spine_description: An AI system vastly better than the best human researcher at AI research.

milestones.name: Top-Expert-Dominating AI
milestones.operational_definition: At least as good as top human experts at virtually all cognitive tasks.
milestones.spine_description: A later AI Futures Model milestone; it does not have a distinct named agent in the frozen AI 2027 ladder.

milestones.name: Artificial Superintelligence
milestones.operational_definition: The gap to the best humans is twice the best-human-to-median-professional gap at virtually all cognitive tasks.
milestones.spine_description: An AI system much better than the best human at every cognitive task.

milestones.name: Wildly Superintelligent AI
milestones.operational_definition: The continuity endpoint of JJ's forecasting framework: wildly superintelligent AI, far past the ASI threshold. Not a standardized AI Futures milestone.
milestones.spine_description: A continuity term rather than a standardized AI Futures milestone.

falsifiers.title: METR 80% time-horizon amendment
falsifiers.statement: Adjudicate the 80% time horizon using TH1.1 or its successor on data published by January 31, 2027.
falsifiers.consequence: At least 10 hours shifts SC and SAR p50 left by 3-6 months; below 5 hours shifts Agent 2 and AC p50 right by 6-9 months; 5-10 hours pushes adjudication.
falsifiers.resolution_protocol: Use METR TH1.1 or its documented successor. Adjudicate on data published by January 31, 2027. At least 10 hours is a pass, 5-10 hours is a push, and below 5 hours is a fail. Record the exact release, task-suite version, and any methodology discontinuity before applying the consequence.

amendment_history.original_statement: SAMPLE - complete wording of the original 100h/50% clause was not supplied.
amendment_history.amendment: Replace the 100h/50% clause with an 80% time-horizon threshold using TH1.1 or its successor.
amendment_history.rationale: The original clause was unadjudicable and may sit above even the fast trajectory.

falsifiers.sample_note: The original amendment text was not supplied verbatim.

falsifiers.title: Continual learning - operational
falsifiers.statement: A deployed frontier system acquires durable new competence from ongoing experience, with weight-level or equivalent adaptation, measured retention, bounded interference, and generalization beyond memorized episodes.
falsifiers.consequence: Earlier Agent 2 and AC; modestly shorter AC-to-SAR if the learning loop is autonomous.
falsifiers.resolution_protocol: SAMPLE - exact source, threshold, adjudication date, and edge-case treatment were not supplied beyond an early-2027 check.
falsifiers.sample_note: The deadline and full resolution protocol are structural placeholders.

falsifiers.title: Novel direction in a hard-to-verify domain
falsifiers.statement: Given a broad objective rather than a supplied agenda, the system originates a non-obvious research direction, designs discriminating experiments, and produces a result that blind domain experts judge novel and valid.
falsifiers.consequence: Collapse the SAR p75-p90 tail toward p50; strongly shorten AC-to-SAR and SAR-to-ASI; treat as major evidence against the widening thesis.
falsifiers.resolution_protocol: Use a hard-to-verify research environment with a broad objective and no supplied agenda. Require blind domain-expert assessment of novelty and validity, public artifacts, and at least one independent replication or equivalent documented audit. Check for benchmark leakage, tool assistance, and hidden human intervention. Lock the adjudicators and edge-case rules before the deadline.

falsifiers.title: Autonomous AI R&D loop
falsifiers.statement: The system proposes, implements, evaluates, interprets, and redirects a sequence of AI experiments with limited human choice points, producing a measured algorithmic gain on held-out evaluations.
falsifiers.consequence: Move SAR and downstream milestones earlier; the effect depends on compute consumed and human oversight.
falsifiers.resolution_protocol: SAMPLE - exact held-out evaluation, gain threshold, oversight ceiling, adjudication date, and edge cases were not supplied.
falsifiers.sample_note: Deadline and protocol details are placeholders.

falsifiers.title: Compute-bound plateau
falsifiers.statement: Large increases in agent labor fail to raise algorithmic progress because decisive experiments are limited by training or inference compute, hardware delivery, power, or validation latency.
falsifiers.consequence: Lengthen SAR-to-ASI and WSI; support widening after SAR.
falsifiers.resolution_protocol: SAMPLE - exact labor increase, progress threshold, measurement window, data source, and edge cases were not supplied.
falsifiers.sample_note: Deadline and quantitative protocol are placeholders.

evidence.source_label: AI Futures Project - Q1 2026 Timelines Update
evidence.summary: Daniel's Automated Coder median moved from late 2029 to mid 2028 after switching to METR Time Horizon v1.1, adding new model evaluations, shortening the estimated doubling time, and lowering the AC horizon requirement.

bottlenecks.name: Continual learning
bottlenecks.mechanism: Persistent memory, frequent offline retraining, online weight updates, self-generated curricula, and improvement of the learning process are different capability thresholds.
bottlenecks.status_change_criteria: Move when a deployed frontier system acquires durable new competence from ongoing experience with measured retention, bounded interference, and generalization beyond memorized episodes.

history.note: SAMPLE - initial status was not supplied.

bottlenecks.sample_note: Mechanism is sourced; current status is a placeholder.

bottlenecks.name: Research taste
bottlenecks.mechanism: Automating perspiration multiplies experiments but does not manufacture research taste to select among them; taste and agenda-setting act as the Amdahl limiter on the serial path to SAR.
bottlenecks.status_change_criteria: A confirmed novel research direction in a hard-to-verify environment by Q4 2028 collapses the SAR p75-p90 tail toward p50 and is major evidence against the widening thesis.

history.note: SAMPLE - initial status was not supplied.

bottlenecks.sample_note: Mechanism and criterion are sourced; current status is a placeholder.

bottlenecks.name: Compute economics
bottlenecks.mechanism: Compute buildout and diffusion into slow-feedback, hard-to-verify domains remain limiters after the research-taste bottleneck has been crossed.
bottlenecks.status_change_criteria: Move when large increases in agent labor fail to raise algorithmic progress because decisive experiments are limited by compute, hardware delivery, power, or validation latency.

history.note: SAMPLE - initial status was not supplied.

bottlenecks.sample_note: Mechanism and criterion are sourced; current status is a placeholder.

bottlenecks.name: Verification asymmetry
bottlenecks.mechanism: Research-domain progress can accelerate fastest where experiments are easy to score, while messy slow-feedback fields remain difficult to train and verify.
bottlenecks.status_change_criteria: Broad expert dominance while research capability remains only SAR-level reverses the SIAR-before-TED-AI ordering and counts against research-domain-first compression.

history.note: SAMPLE - initial status was not supplied.

bottlenecks.sample_note: Mechanism and criterion are sourced; current status is a placeholder.
